reported_entities.name: Apple
reported_entities.claim: TSMC's largest customer for years, ~22-25% of TSMC revenue; uses TSMC's most advanced nodes for A-series and M-series chips.

reported_entities.name: Nvidia
reported_entities.claim: Set to supplant Apple as TSMC's top customer in 2026, driven by AI GPU demand (~11% of revenue per earlier estimates, rising).

reported_entities.name: AMD
reported_entities.claim: Top-3 TSMC customer (~7% of revenue); fabless GPU/CPU maker relying on TSMC's advanced nodes.

reported_entities.name: Qualcomm
reported_entities.claim: One of TSMC's six major clients (~8% of revenue) named among top customers seeking Samsung/Intel as capacity backup.

reported_entities.name: MediaTek
reported_entities.claim: Major TSMC customer (~9-10% of revenue) and one of the first customers confirmed for TSMC's 2nm node.

reported_entities.name: Broadcom
reported_entities.claim: One of TSMC's six major clients (~11-15% of revenue estimate for 2026), largely for custom AI ASICs.

reported_entities.name: Intel
reported_entities.claim: Increasingly outsources some chip production to TSMC (~7% of TSMC revenue estimated for 2026) while also being a foundry competitor.

reported_entities.name: ARM
reported_entities.claim: Listed by Wikipedia among fabless semiconductor companies that are customers of TSMC.

reported_entities.name: Marvell
reported_entities.claim: Listed by Wikipedia among fabless semiconductor companies that are customers of TSMC.

notes: Salva run post-fix still returned qualified_count=0 despite retrieval_health='ok' and no OMLX errors surfaced beyond the expected fallback warning -- the scoring-layer fix for find_partnership_signals did not surface any qualified entities in this rerun, so entities list was empty and provided no candidates. Supplemented entirely via 2 WebSearch queries (CNBC/BigGo/TechPowerUp/Wikipedia/TweakTown) to compile the well-documented major customer list: Apple, Nvidia, AMD, Qualcomm, MediaTek, Broadcom, Intel (as outsourcing customer), ARM, Marvell.
